# sendgrid_subuser_whitelabel_domain (Resource)

Associates an authenticated domain with a single SendGrid subuser.

This resource is a thin wrapper around the SendGrid "associate authenticated domain with a subuser" API.
The parent account must first authenticate and validate the domain before association.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "sendgrid_sender_authentication" "example" {
  domain = "example.com"
}

resource "sendgrid_subuser" "example" {
  username = "example-subuser"
  email    = "subuser@example.com"
  password = "SecurePassword123!"
  ips      = ["192.0.2.1"]
}

resource "sendgrid_subuser_whitelabel_domain" "example" {
  domain_id = sendgrid_sender_authentication.example.id
  subuser   = sendgrid_subuser.example.username
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `domain_id` (Number) The authenticated domain ID to associate with the subuser.
- `subuser` (String) The subuser username to associate with the authenticated domain.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Synthetic ID of this association in the form "<domain_id>:<subuser>".

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using subuser username only
terraform import sendgrid_subuser_whitelabel_domain.example example-subuser

# Import using domain_id:subuser format
terraform import sendgrid_subuser_whitelabel_domain.example 12345:example-subuser
```
